This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-boosting properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, gastrointestinal disorders, and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Cat's Claw include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, such as oxindole alkaloids, isopteropodine, and mitraphylline, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Cat's Claw include teas, capsules, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
